heroHeadline: CAPE Tariff Refund Portal Opens (System Crashed Day 1); Warsh Senate Hearing; Oil Back to $95 — FOMC 8 Days Away
heroSubtitle: Apr 21: Kevin Warsh's Senate Banking confirmation hearing as Fed Chair nominee (replacing Powell, whose term expires May 15) dominated the macro agenda on a day the S&P 500 recovered 0.21% to 7,124.28. WTI crude re-escalated to ~$95.75/bbl (Brent $96.32) — fully reversing the April 17 crash relief from $85.57 — as Trump expressed optimism about a US-Iran deal before the April 23 ceasefire deadline but uncertainty persisted. The CAPE tariff refund portal ($166B IEEPA refunds) launched April 20 but crashed under high volume on Day 1. IEA: March 2026 oil supply disruption of 10.1 mbpd was the largest in history. Moody's Analytics: 49% US recession probability. FOMC April 29-30 stagflation decision and ECB April 30 rate decision now 8 days away.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Semafor
